content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree of NNN nodes, and 222 arrays of size NNN - AAA and BBB.

You would like to ensure that Ai≠BiA_i \neq B_iAi​=Bi​ for every 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N.

To achieve this, you can perform the following operation **exactly once**:


Pick a subset S={X1,X2,…,X∣S∣}S = \{X_1, X_2, \ldots, X_{|S|}\}S={X1​,X2​,…,X∣S∣​} of the nodes, such that the subgraph induced by SSS is **connected**.

In other words, if x,y∈Sx, y \in Sx,y∈S, then every vertex on the (unique) path connecting xxx and yyy should also belong to SSS.


Then, rearrange the values AX1,AX2,…,AX∣S∣A_{X_1}, A_{X_2}, \ldots, A_{X_{|S|}}AX1​​,AX2​​,…,AX∣S∣​​ among positions X1,X2,…,X∣S∣X_1, X_2, \ldots, X_{|S|}X1​,X2​,…,X∣S∣​, however you like.
Find the **minimum** size of the subset SSS, and the number of ways to choose a minimum-sized subset SSS, such that it is possible to rearrange and meet the condition.

Since the number of ways may be large, output it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

**Note:**


Choosing the empty subset is allowed. You can assume there exists exactly one way to choose an empty subset.
There may not exist any valid subset you can pick. In that case, output −1−1-1 -1−1−1.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of input contains NNN - the size of the tree
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers - B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​.
The next N−1N - 1N−1 lines contain 222 space-separated integers each, uuu and vvv, denoting an edge between uuu and vvv in the tree.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line 222 space-separated integers: first the minimum possible size of SSS, and second the number of ways to choose a minimum-sized SSS modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


In case no valid subset exists, output −1−1-1 -1−1−1.
constraints: The given set of edges describes a tree.
The sum of N2N^2N2 over all test cases does not exceed 16⋅10616 \cdot 10^616⋅106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: It can be proven that the minimum sized subset needed is 222. There are two ways to choose such a subset:

Choose S={1,3}S = \{1, 3\}S={1,3}, and then swap A1A_1A1​ and A3A_3A3​.
Choose S={1,4}S = \{1, 4\}S={1,4} and then swap A1A_1A1​ and A4A_4A4​.

It can be verified that Ai≠BiA_i \ne B_iAi​=Bi​ for every iii in both cases.
On the other hand,S={1,2}S = \{1, 2\}S={1,2} is not good as it's not possible to find a valid rearrangement.
